title: query_base success response envelope
description: Success response shape for the query_base typed MCP tool. Authoritative source is the Zod output schema in src/tools/query_base/schema.ts; this JSON Schema is the human-readable cross-reference. Errors are NOT represented here — they surface through UpstreamError thrown from the handler and serialised by the MCP SDK's error-response shape (see contracts/errors.md).

columns.description: Column names in view-declared order. The reserved key 'path' always appears at index 0. A view-defined column named 'path' is renamed to 'path_view' (FR-002b) and appears in this array at the index the view declared for the original 'path' column. Populated even when 'rows' is the empty array (FR-002c) so the agent learns the view's schema from a zero-row response.

path.description: Reserved wrapper-guaranteed source-note locator (FR-002a). Vault-relative path. Always present, regardless of whether the view's column selection includes file.path — synthesised by the wrapper when upstream omits it.

additionalProperties.description: All other row keys are passthrough from upstream (FR-002d). Wrapper does not synthesise, suppress, or type-coerce them. Native JSON types preserved (FR-014): string, number, boolean, null, nested object, ISO-date string. The key names match the view's declared column names; insertion order matches the 'columns' vector as a secondary guarantee.

rows.description: Up to 1000 row objects, ordered per FR-003 (view's explicit sort as primary, then row.path ascending as tiebreaker, then upstream emission for path-equal rows).

truncated.description: Truncation signal (FR-013). Always present. false when the view's full row set fit within the 1000-row cap. true when the wrapper sliced the row set down.

total_rows.description: Present only when truncated: true. Reports the upstream's full match count for the view at query time so the caller can plan narrowing strategy without iterative probing. Omitted when truncated: false (the count is rows.length, no separate field needed). Minimum 1001 because total_rows is only emitted when the count exceeds the 1000-row cap.

examples.$comment: Empty view — successful zero-row response (FR-006). columns still populated.
Truncated response — view matched 4527 notes total, first 1000 returned (FR-013).
